violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment has employees certified through a food safety management course. One employee must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

Sanitizer bucket was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above/adjacent to food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients were not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The door to the outside, located in receiving, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2026
Flooring in areas throughout the facility is in poor repair, with cracked tiles and unsealed cement. Per interview of manager, remodel is expected soon. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2023
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

Sanitizer buckets observed stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by locating them in an area that is not above/beside food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

The receiving door is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
Flooring in areas throughout the facility is in poor repair, with cracked tiles and unsealed cement. Per interview of manager, remodel is expected next year. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2023
Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies observed not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2025

Sanitizer buckets were stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles. Chemicals must be stored below any food/equipment/single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2025

The receiving door is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

2-drawer cold hold unit is not in good repair. Large amount of ice build-up observed. These drawers are not currently being used. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The floor to the side and in the front of the walk-in cooler, near the receiving door, and behind the donut case, is in poor repair. Tiles are cracked and the area in front of the walk-in is concrete/cement. These areas are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2023

Working containers of sugar are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
Refrigerator door gasket of lowboy unit #3 observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket replaced during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024

The floor to the side and in the front of the walk-in cooler, near the receiving door, and behind the donut case, is in poor repair. Tiles are cracked and the area in front of the walk-in is concrete/cement. These areas are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2023
